summary: Jack Henry filed an 8-K on 2026-03-26 disclosing entry into one material agreement, termination of another, and a new financial obligation; exhibit content unknown.
Fiserv engaged Cognition AI as a second AI modernization partner alongside its existing OpenAI/agentOS relationship.
CFPB Supervision Division announced a procedural 'Humility Pledge' changing exam conduct under new leadership; substantive impact on data-aggregation supervision unclear.
Jack Henry launched Bud Financial-powered transaction enrichment as a native Banno feature, now shipping across ~1,000 FIs in the Jack Henry footprint.
Woodforest National Bank ($9B AUM, 740 branches) selected Jack Henry as its core platform, expanding Banno's reachable FI footprint.
Socure named Chung-Man Tam as CPO and Pablo Abreu as Chief AI and Innovation Officer, signaling potential product direction shift at a verify/auth competitor.
New York proposed open banking-style financial data access legislation, creating a state-level parallel track to the stalled federal CFPB Section 1033 rulemaking.
Jack Henry filed an 8-K disclosing a new material agreement, termination of another, and a new financial obligation — unusual three-item combination at ASA's primary distribution channel.
Fiserv formally announced a platform-wide OpenAI partnership to embed frontier AI across financial services platforms, escalating the prior agentOS pilot.
Socure integrated Prism Data cash-flow underwriting signals into RiskOS, extending a channel partner into territory overlapping Verify income signals and Forecast recurring-flow modeling.
OpenAI and Plaid expanded ChatGPT's Plaid-connected PFM from Pro-tier preview to general consumer product, escalating disintermediation risk to FI-channel PFM surfaces.
CFPB allocated $46M to consumers harmed by Synapse/Evolve middleware collapse, the first regulator-funded fintech remediation of its kind.
Q2 Holdings partnered with Stablecore to offer stablecoin payments to bank and credit union clients, introducing a new payment rail inside an ASA distribution channel.
Nymbus shipped a secure MCP server for AI-driven core banking actions, becoming the third MCP-native core/channel vendor after Personetics and Bud.
Fiserv disclosed non-core asset divestitures via 8-K; divested units unnamed, with potential impact on digital banking or aggregation channels.
Live Oak Bank booked a $24M Q3 gain on sale of its Apiture stake, providing a concrete valuation datapoint separate from the CSI acquisition narrative.
FinCEN proposed rule revising baseline AML program requirements for all FIs, distinct from GENIUS Act AML NPRM; affects every ASA channel-partner FI.
Plaid launched Guaranteed Payments, a fraud-graph-backed managed ACH approval product moving Plaid into balance-sheet-adjacent payment assurance.
Plaid's Effects 2026 event announced a broad wave of launches spanning AI, fraud, lending, payments, and developer tooling in a single release cycle.
Plaid launched Cash Advance Index, a repayment risk score derived from network and cash flow data aimed at lender decisioning.
CFPB formally notified a court it cannot lawfully draw Federal Reserve funds under Dodd-Frank, signaling operational defunding distinct from prior Sixth Circuit pause.
CFPB shifted Section 1033 to full notice-and-comment rulemaking after credit-union lobbying, extending compliance-timeline ambiguity into 2027.
Bud Financial disclosed sub-5ms transaction processing on IBM watsonx.data, the first concrete infrastructure performance claim from the Banno-embedded vendor.
Trump EO directing Fed and other agencies to study fintech rail access; Banking Dive adds expanded agency scope beyond prior reporting.
MX Technologies named Wes Hummel CTO, completing a full C-suite reset with CEO Magats within roughly twelve months.
FDX's CFPB-recognized open banking standard-setter status reaffirmed as de facto compliance target while CFPB rulemaking stalls.
Sixth Circuit paused all appeals challenging CFPB's Section 1033 rule pending rulemaking, extending regulatory ambiguity into late 2026.
Trump signed an executive order directing the Fed to study expanding fintech access to payment rails, signaling administration favor for non-bank rail access.
FinCEN and OFAC proposed AML and sanctions program requirements implementing the GENIUS Act, creating new compliance obligations for platforms touching stablecoin flows.
Fiserv embedded Affirm BNPL directly into debit card products, requiring ASA Pay to handle debit-originated installments in transaction taxonomy.
Socure and Thomson Reuters announced an AI-driven digital identity and fraud-prevention partnership, bundling Socure identity with Thomson Reuters regulatory and risk datasets.
Jack Henry filed a second Item 5.02 8-K disclosing a director/officer change, continuing a pattern of governance filings within 12 months.
eMarketer commentary framed Fiserv's OpenAI collaboration as targeting banks' biggest operational headaches; no new product or partner facts beyond the May 14 agentOS launch.
Corelation's KeyStone core signed $59B State Employees' Credit Union, materially expanding the credit-union TAM accessible via ASA's Corelation channel integration.
Alkami filed an 8-K disclosing a material definitive agreement and unregistered equity sale; counterparty undisclosed.
ChatGPT personal finance suite entered preview rollout via Plaid integration, escalating from announcement to shipping product for account connection and spend/planning queries.
Lumin Digital's Solaire AI-native intelligence layer received second-source confirmation with no scope expansion beyond prior coverage.
Fiserv agentOS agentic AI operating system for banking workflows received multiple second-source coverage cycles with no new product or partner facts beyond the May 14 launch.
Bud Financial's First Fidelity Bank deployment was second-sourced via Fintech Times with no new FI count or deal terms disclosed.
Plaid is building a proprietary transaction foundation model for categorization and insights, directly contesting One View's enrichment differentiation.
Plaid expanded its Perplexity integration to surface net worth, spend tracking, and AI-driven financial planning insights.
Plaid launched a new Income engine with finer taxonomy and configurable calculation controls, raising the feature bar in income verification.
Plaid expanded Bank Intelligence with Fraud Insights and a Primacy Score loyalty metric targeting FI-facing decisioning, overlapping Compass and One View.
JPMorgan signed executed fee agreements with multiple fintech aggregators, converting data-access costs from speculation to contracted reality and resetting Vault unit economics.
CFPB will not prioritize enforcement of its 2024 BNPL Reg Z digital-accounts rule, easing near-term compliance burden for BNPL-adjacent payment flows.
Plaid is now the default consumer-permissioned data rail for frontier AI platforms, combining data connectivity and intelligence layers for third-party AI surfaces.
OpenAI launched a Plaid-powered personal finance dashboard inside ChatGPT for Pro users on May 15, directly competing with Compass and One View.
Two additional Q2 Holdings 8-Ks (Items 5.02) extend leadership-churn pattern; cumulative four filings now on record.
OCC preempted Illinois's interchange fee law, federalizing payment-fee rulemaking and reshaping legal terrain for ASA Pay fee and rev-share structures.
Jack Henry 8-K (Item 5.02) discloses a second undisclosed director/officer change event; counterparty and role still not public.
